name: ViewBasedImplementation
description: Complete guide for implementing Lola Framework UI using the view-based pattern with themes and layouts

installation.note: Import CSS in main.tsx or index.tsx

colorConfig.description: Color configuration object that the framework understands. Always use a variable named 'colorConfig'

properties.title: Title color
properties.subtitle: Subtitle color
properties.textColorBtn: Button text color (always required when using Button)

pattern.contentContainer: Use div or section with flexbox, NOT Grid (Grid doesn't exist)
pattern.overflowHandling: Use isOverflowauto={true} on Layout.Content when content is large

layoutStructure.example:
```tsx
{/* content based on view type */}
```
layoutStructure.importantNote: Always use isOverflowauto={true} on Layout.Content when content might be tall or dynamically sized

primaryMeshGradientView.example:
```tsx
const colorConfig = theme.primaryMeshGradientView;

<AuraLayout colorConfig={colorConfig}>
  <Layout.Content>
    <ElevatedCircle>
      <RightIcon size={66} />
    </ElevatedCircle>
    <PageTitle
      highlight="Main Title"
      highlightColor={colorConfig.title}
      secondary="Subtitle"
      secondaryColor={colorConfig.subtitle}
    />
    <BodyCopy textColor={colorConfig.bodyCopy}>
      Content text
    </BodyCopy>
  </Layout.Content>
</AuraLayout>
```

errorView.example:
```tsx
const colorConfig = theme.errorView;

<AuraLayout colorConfig={colorConfig}>
  <Layout.Content>
    <ElevatedCircle>
      <WarningIcon size={66} />
    </ElevatedCircle>
    <PageTitle
      highlight="Error Title"
      highlightColor={colorConfig.title}
    />
    <BodyCopy textColor={colorConfig.bodyCopy}>
      Error description
    </BodyCopy>
  </Layout.Content>
</AuraLayout>
```

dataView.example:
```tsx
const colorConfig = theme.dataView;

<AuraLayout colorConfig={colorConfig}>
  <Layout.Content>
    <section className="content">
      {/* Data display components */}
    </section>
  </Layout.Content>
</AuraLayout>
```

Button.example:
```tsx
<Button
  showIcon
  size="large"
  background={colorConfig.backgroundBtn}
  color={colorConfig.textColorBtn}
>
  Button Text
</Button>
```

PageTitle.example:
```tsx
<PageTitle
  highlight="Main Title"
  highlightColor={colorConfig.title}
  secondary="Subtitle Text"
  secondaryColor={colorConfig.subtitle}
/>
```

BodyCopy.example:
```tsx
<BodyCopy style={{ color: colorConfig.bodyCopy }}>
  Your content text here
</BodyCopy>
```
BodyCopy.note: Do NOT use textColor prop - it causes React warnings. Use style={{ color: '...' }} instead.

SearchSelect.note: CRITICAL: SearchSelect onChange passes the entire item object {code, label}, not just the code. You MUST extract the code property or you'll get 'Objects are not valid as a React child' error.

completeExample.code:
```tsx
import {
  AuraLayout,
  Layout,
  Navbar,
  PageTitle,
  BodyCopy,
  Button,
  ElevatedCircle,
  Grid
} from 'lola-framework-ui-test';
import { RightIcon } from 'lola-framework-ui-test/icons';

function MyView({ theme }) {
  const colorConfig = theme.primaryMeshGradientView;

  return (
    <AuraLayout colorConfig={colorConfig}>
      <Layout.Header>
        <Navbar
          color={colorConfig.title}
          title="Welcome"
        />
      </Layout.Header>

      <Layout.Content>
        <Grid>
          <ElevatedCircle>
            <RightIcon size={66} />
          </ElevatedCircle>
          
          <PageTitle
            highlight="Get Started"
            highlightColor={colorConfig.title}
            secondary="Begin your journey"
            secondaryColor={colorConfig.subtitle}
          />
          
          <BodyCopy textColor={colorConfig.bodyCopy}>
            Welcome to the application. Follow the steps to continue.
          </BodyCopy>
        </Grid>
      </Layout.Content>

      <Layout.Footer>
        <Button
          showIcon
          size="large"
          background={colorConfig.backgroundBtn}
          color={colorConfig.textColorBtn}
        >
          Continue
        </Button>
      </Layout.Footer>
    </AuraLayout>
  );
}
```

colorConfig: Always define colorConfig at the component level
Get colorConfig from theme object based on view type
Use colorConfig properties for all color values
structure: Match content structure to view type
components: Always provide required color props from colorConfig
Use Button's showIcon and size props appropriately

errors.error: Cannot destructure property 'themeType' of 'e' as it is undefined
errors.solution: Always pass colorConfig prop to AuraLayout

errors.mistake: Using inline styles or custom CSS

errors.solution: Follow the standard layout structure pattern

errors.mistake: Hardcoding colors instead of using colorConfig
errors.solution: Always use colors from colorConfig object
